# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3'  # define version of the docker-compose file using a specific syntax (v2 or v3)
services:   # list all services in this part, each service runs one instance at any time and can also be shared among multiple users. It is recommended to place related containers under common namespaces like 'app' for readability – not required but good practice!    
    elasticsearch:       # define a new named ElasticSearch container here with the tag name "elastic"   (default image)        docker run --name some-es -p 9201:9200 -d elastics/elasticsearch
      restart: 'always'                
                            mem_limit : memory for elastic service in GB. Recommended range is around a factor of ~3 to start with          # set the restarts policy as always         logging driver   docker logs --follow containername or simply “c” command if you want details           stdout,stderr      To enable detaching log from running process and keeping output files: enabled      
    kibana:             
        ports:                      - "5601"                       # Map port to another (or set it as the default)            expose_mode          Forwarded Ports         Kibana exposes http-interactive requests on these addresses. Use 82 if you prefer a stable ip           mem_limit : same here                  
    logstash:                hostname             ports   container_port     protocol      Both dockerized and nondockerized logs are exposed Here we define how to expose the logging service of LogStash, by mapping it’s port or address. In both cases Kibana UI will be available at http://localhost/5601 in your browser                    
    logstash-forwarder:              ports           container_port      protocol          forwarded logs            enabled                   stdout                 enable verbosity for logged requests  LogStashes Forwarders logging is a bit more intricate here, since they are designed to handle high volume of data and require complex parsing logic which may slow down the process.
